title: HxLibPkg Package Specification
description: JSON specification file used by `node cli.js create-package --spec <file>` to build a .hxlibpkg library package. File paths inside this document are resolved relative to the directory containing the spec file itself. Unknown properties are preserved to support forward compatibility with future application versions.

library_name.description: Name of the library. If omitted, auto-detected from the first .hsl, .hs_, or .smt file in library_files (in that priority order). Must not contain path separators.

author.description: Full name of the library author. Required.

version.description: Library version number string. Required. No specific format is enforced, but semantic versioning is recommended.

venus_compatibility.description: VENUS software version compatibility range or description. Required.

description.description: Human-readable description of the library's purpose, features, and usage notes. Required.
examples: Provides high-level pipetting helpers for the ML600 syringe pump.

github_url.description: URL to the library's GitHub repository. Optional.

tags.description: Searchable keyword tags for the library. Optional.

library_image.description: Path to an image file used as the library icon (.png, .jpg, .bmp, .svg, .gif, .ico). Resolved relative to the spec file directory. If omitted, the packager looks for a .bmp whose basename matches library_name among library_files.

library_files.description: Paths to the library payload files (.hsl, .hs_, .hsi, .smt, .stp, .dll, .bmp, and any other distributed files). At least one file is required. Paths are resolved relative to the spec file directory.

demo_method_files.description: Paths to demo / example method files (.hsl, .lay, .med, etc.) that illustrate how to use the library. Installed separately under the Methods directory. Optional.

labware_files.description: Paths to labware definition files (.rck, .ctr, .tml, .dck, .lay) that ship with the library. Each entry is a relative path with an optional subdirectory under the Labware root. File paths must be purely relative — no drive letters, no '//' or '\\', and must begin with one of the three allowed roots (Library, Methods, Labware) or be bare filenames. Installed under the Hamilton Labware directory. Optional.

help_files.description: Basenames of .chm help files that ship with the library. These are installed in the library folder alongside library_files but tracked separately so the UI can surface them for quick access. If omitted, .chm files are auto-detected from library_files during import.

default_help_file.description: Basename of the .chm help file to open by default when the user clicks Help on the library card. Only meaningful when the library contains multiple .chm help files. When set, clicking Help opens this file directly instead of showing a menu of all help files. Must match a basename in help_files (or a .chm in library_files).

com_register_dlls.description: Basenames (not full paths) of DLL files listed in library_files that must be registered as COM objects using RegAsm.exe /codebase. Administrator rights are required at import time. DLL names must exactly match basenames of entries in library_files.

install_to_library_root.description: When true, library files are installed directly into the Library root directory instead of a subdirectory named after the library. Some libraries require this for compatibility. The user can override this setting at import time.

installer_executable.description: Path to an optional standalone installer executable (.exe) for this library. Resolved relative to the spec file directory. The installer is embedded in the package under the installer/ directory and can be extracted to a centralized installer store on import when the retain-installers setting is enabled. This is intended for libraries that ship their own setup programs.

installer_info.description: Optional metadata about the bundled installer executable.

source_url.description: URL where the installer was originally downloaded from.

authenticode_signer.description: Authenticode signer name from the .exe digital signature, if known.
